title: Touch the test set exactly once
description: Reserve a test split that is never used for tuning, model selection, or early stopping, and evaluate on it a single time at the end.
body: Use when reporting any performance number that claims to predict real-world behavior. Do not tune hyperparameters, pick architectures, or early-stop against the test set — that turns it into a second validation set and inflates the reported metric. Actions: split into train/validation/test before any modeling, tune only on validation, then run the test set once and record the number with the run id. Validation signals include a test metric close to the final validation metric and an audit log showing the test split was read once. Risk boundary: a test number used for selection is a leaked metric; if the test set was touched during tuning, escalate and collect a fresh held-out set before publishing.
